report_name: SeasonalInvite Phishing Campaign Abuses Legitimate RMM Tools

threat_actors.name: SeasonalInvite Actor
threat_actors.description: Unidentified threat actor behind the SeasonalInvite campaign, demonstrating sophistication in infrastructure management and social engineering, including the use of a Traffic Distribution System and potentially AI-assisted techniques for lure generation.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The SeasonalInvite campaign uses phishing emails with lures related to seasonal eCards, containing a link that directs the user to a malicious phishing website.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: The campaign abuses trusted, legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools like ConnectWise, LogMeIn, and Kaseya to gain persistent remote access to victim systems, bypassing traditional security controls.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Victims are tricked into downloading and executing an installer from a phishing website, which installs a legitimate RMM agent on their system.

attack_patterns.name: Generative AI
attack_patterns.description: The campaign reportedly uses AI-assisted techniques for generating convincing phishing lures and managing its complex infrastructure.

tools.name: ConnectWise ScreenConnect
tools.description: A legitimate RMM tool abused by the SeasonalInvite campaign to gain persistent remote access to compromised systems.

tools.name: LogMeIn Resolve
tools.description: A legitimate RMM tool abused by the SeasonalInvite campaign to gain persistent remote access to compromised systems.

tools.name: Kaseya
tools.description: A legitimate RMM platform abused by the SeasonalInvite campaign to gain persistent remote access to compromised systems.

tools.name: O&O Syspectr
tools.description: A legitimate RMM tool abused by the SeasonalInvite campaign to gain persistent remote access to compromised systems.

campaigns.name: SeasonalInvite
campaigns.description: A sophisticated phishing campaign active since at least January 2026 that uses fake seasonal eCard invitations to trick users into installing legitimate RMM tools for persistent remote access.

infrastructure.name: SeasonalInvite Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A large infrastructure of 959 domains used to host phishing pages for the SeasonalInvite campaign.

infrastructure.name: SeasonalInvite Traffic Distribution System
infrastructure.description: A Traffic Distribution System (TDS) with over 2,600 gate pages used by the SeasonalInvite campaign to filter victims and evade detection by security researchers and bots.

indicators.name: SeasonalInvite RMM Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: SeasonalInvite RMM Tool Execution
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e7f-4b2a-9c1d-0e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of RMM client processes associated with the SeasonalInvite campaign.
references:
  - https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/14/seasonalinvite-phishing-campaign-rmm-tools/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1219
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ScreenConnect.Client.exe'
      - '\LogMeIn.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of legitimate RMM tools such as ScreenConnect and LogMeIn, which are abused in the SeasonalInvite campaign. Alerts should be investigated for unauthorized installations, especially on non-IT staff systems.

indicators.name: SeasonalInvite Lure File Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: SeasonalInvite Lure File Execution
id: f8a7b6c5-4d3e-4f2a-8b1c-9d0e8f7a6b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of files with names used as lures in the SeasonalInvite phishing campaign.
references:
  - https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/14/seasonalinvite-phishing-campaign-rmm-tools/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.t1204.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\eCard.exe'
      - '\invitation.scr'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of executables with names like 'eCard.exe' or 'invitation.scr', which are used as lures in the SeasonalInvite campaign to trick users into installing malicious software.

indicators.name: SeasonalInvite RMM Network Connections
indicators.pattern:
title: SeasonalInvite RMM Network Connections
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to known RMM service domains that may indicate unauthorized RMM tool usage as seen in the SeasonalInvite campaign.
references:
  - https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/14/seasonalinvite-phishing-campaign-rmm-tools/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1219
level: low
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - '.screenconnect.com'
      - '.logmein.com'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\SomeLegitITTool.exe' # Add legitimate IT tools to filter false positives
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects network connections to known RMM service domains like '*.screenconnect.com' and '*.logmein.com'. Such connections from non-IT assets could indicate a compromise by the SeasonalInvite campaign.
